title: Indonesia updates food-contact packaging rules with paper and board in scope
summary: Food Packaging Forum reports that Indonesia's BPOM adopted Regulation No. 11 of 2026 on food packaging, replacing the 2019 rule. The regulation covers paper and board as well as plastics, metals, glass, multilayer materials and other food-contact materials, with overall and specific migration requirements, positive and prohibited substance lists, and provisions for reusable and recycled packaging.
buyerTakeaway: For Southeast Asia-bound bakery paper, greaseproof wraps and printed food paper packs, confirm whether the finished item needs migration evidence, substance-list checks and updated supplier declarations before the 12-month transition window closes.

title: Metsa Board expands paperboard design support for faster compliant packaging work
summary: Packaging Insights reports that Metsa Board has opened a Milan packaging design studio that combines material expertise, AI-supported simulations, prototyping and design review. The studio is intended to help food, pharma and beauty brands assess lighter, recyclable and regulatory-compliant packaging options earlier in development.
buyerTakeaway: For bakery and foodservice paperboard projects, involve the board supplier before artwork lock. Early trials on material efficiency, converting, barrier needs and recyclability evidence can reduce redesigns after compliance review.
imageAlt: Metsa Board Milan packaging design studio image published with Packaging Insights' report

title: European Commission updates EUDR scope and digital tools before year-end application
summary: The European Commission adopted measures to support EUDR implementation, including an updated product-scope Delegated Act and technical rules for the Information System used for due diligence statements and simplified declarations. The Commission says the changes are intended to give businesses more legal certainty before application begins at the end of 2026.
buyerTakeaway: For EU paper packaging orders, re-check whether the SKU is sold as a covered product or only used as carrier packaging, and prepare supplier-origin, fiber and due-diligence records in a format that can support the EUDR Information System.

title: Molded fiber growth faces recyclable-labeling and coating evidence questions
summary: Packaging Dive reports that molded fiber producers expect growth as brands replace single-use plastics, while California's SB 343 recyclable-labeling rules may create end-of-life claims pressure. The article also notes that PFAS, barriers and coatings remain important regulatory considerations for molded fiber packaging.
buyerTakeaway: For bakery trays, foodservice inserts and fiber takeout formats, treat molded fiber as a specification project, not only a plastic substitute. Confirm recyclability claims by sales region and document any grease barrier, coating and PFAS status.

title: FDA decision on PFAS in food raises the bar for packaging evidence
summary: The Guardian reports that the U.S. FDA rejected a petition seeking enforceable PFAS limits in food while indicating it may work on action levels. The article also notes that PFAS can enter the food system through several routes, including packaging, making documented source control important for food brands.
buyerTakeaway: U.S. buyers should keep finished-item PFAS documentation for greaseproof paper, baking paper and foodservice packs even where federal food thresholds remain unsettled. Supplier letters should be backed by formulation controls or testing, not only marketing claims.
imageAlt: Article image from The Guardian's report on the FDA decision about PFAS limits in food

title: Dessert packaging buyers prioritize freshness, resealability and recyclability
summary: Packaging Insights reports that dessert and sweet snack packs are being redesigned around product protection and circularity. Suppliers point to resealable formats, barrier performance, easy opening and recyclable monomaterial structures as brands work to protect texture while meeting consumer and regulatory expectations.
buyerTakeaway: For bakery cookies, muffins and sweet snack paper formats, define the product-protection target before changing the pack. Ask suppliers to document grease and moisture control, seal performance, recyclability claims, print system and line compatibility together.
imageAlt: Cookie packaging image published with Packaging Insights' report on dessert packaging trends

title: Food-contact ink guidance reinforces migration control for printed paper packs
summary: Packaging Technology Today explains that food-contact packaging materials must be selected with migration, odor, substrate behavior and regulatory documentation in mind. The article highlights migration-optimized inks as one way to reduce set-off and contamination risk when printed packs sit close to food.
buyerTakeaway: For custom cupcake liners, greaseproof wraps and bakery cartons, ask suppliers to identify the ink system, intended food-contact condition, migration assumptions and odor-control checks before approving artwork for production.
imageAlt: Printed food packaging image published with Packaging Technology Today's article on food-contact material selection

title: New organic-fluorine screening method sharpens PPWR evidence planning
summary: Norner has introduced a Total Organic Fluorine screening method using pyrolysis-GC/MS to distinguish PFAS-relevant organic fluorine from inorganic fluorine in solid materials. The method is positioned for coatings, composites and products with uncertain PFAS histories, with targeted PFAS analysis added where the screening result or risk profile requires it.
buyerTakeaway: For EU-bound grease-resistant paper and coated food packaging, agree the screening method, reporting limit, sample construction and escalation to targeted PFAS testing before production. A generic PFAS-free statement is not a substitute for finished-item evidence.
imageAlt: Laboratory testing image published with Packaging Insights' report on PFAS screening

title: Official guidance puts the EU and Northern Ireland BPA deadline into buyer terms
summary: Food Standards Scotland says most newly manufactured or imported food-contact materials first placed on the EU or Northern Ireland market from 20 July 2026 must meet the EU restrictions on intentionally used BPA and other hazardous bisphenols, subject to defined transitions. It also distinguishes this requirement from the current position in Great Britain.
buyerTakeaway: For coated, printed or bonded bakery paper supplied to the EU or Northern Ireland, confirm market-placement dates and obtain current supplier assurances, declarations and supporting purity or test data. Do not treat a GB-only compliance position as sufficient.

title: Potato-starch barriers target PFAS-free grease resistance for bakery paper
summary: Royal Avebe presented its plant-based barrier systems for fiber food packaging at Barrier Coatings for Food Packaging 2026. PerfectaFILM is designed to limit oil and grease penetration in paper, while PerfectaCOL combines heat-pressure sealing with barrier performance for dry foods, baked goods and foodservice formats.
buyerTakeaway: Ask coating suppliers for finished-paper grease resistance, food-contact status, BfR/FDA support, coat-weight targets and sealing trials on the actual base paper. A bio-based ingredient claim alone does not prove production performance or finished-pack compliance.
imageAlt: Royal Avebe image for its plant-based barrier coatings for food packaging
